behavior_summary: Provides the read/use handlers for a set of in-game magic scrolls. The primary behavior (U8P_SCROLL2::func0080) implements the 'Scroll of Dispelling Magical Portals': it displays the scroll text, freezes the avatar while effects/animations run, scans nearby world objects/items for sealed/portal/door objects (identified by item quality/status bits), attempts to clear a sealing bit on qualifying items and then invokes the door-use routine to open/unseal them, and plays visual/sound effects and short player barks when no applicable targets are found.

Several other scroll handlers follow the same read/stasis/animation pattern for detection, trap destruction, healing, restoration, invisibility and revealing treasure. These handlers spawn short-lived helper processes to animate and/or perform the substantive effect; many concrete spell effects are performed by spawned methods or external processes rather than fully in-script.

Helper routines create/pop temporary indicator items and step item frames to produce visible animations (U8P_SCROLL2::func042D, U8P_SCROLL2::func047E, U8P_SCROLL2::func0755). The script uses spawn/suspend/process_result extensively to sequence asynchronous visual effects and to detect success/failure of spawned actions.
notable_control_flow: Most scroll handlers: close UI (FREE::closeAllGumps) -> spawn process Scroll::read (show text) -> setAvatarInStasis(1) -> spawn animation/effect helpers (FREE::func1C19, FREE::func29D8, METHOD::func15E7) with suspend between them -> perform domain action (scan items or spawn helper) -> check process_result() to decide success -> clear stasis and cleanup.

U8P_SCROLL2::func0080 identifies portal targets by testing an item quality mask (low byte masked equals 0x00FA). When a target matches and a local gating condition is true, it clears the 0x0400 sealing bit in the item's quality and then spawns the target's DOOR_NS::useDoor(0x00) to open/unseal it. If the gating condition is false, it emits a player bark recommending another item.

Animation/indicator helpers (U8P_SCROLL2::func042D, U8P_SCROLL2::func047E, U8P_SCROLL2::func0755) perform small loops that advance Item::getFrame/Item::setFrame with spawn/suspend delays and create/pop/destroy temporary items to give the player visual feedback.

Trap-destruction and detection flows call an internal helper (U8P_SCROLL2::func0A9A and U8P_SCROLL2::func0755, respectively) and interpret (word)process_result() to set local success flags and choose between success cleanup or 'none found' barks.
open_questions: Exact semantics of individual quality/status bits (e.g., low-byte 0x00FA and the 0x0400 sealing bit) are inferred from patterns; the canonical meaning in engine data is not present in the script.
Which world objects/types exactly match the portal/chest/trap predicates (the script masks/compares quality words but the type-level filtering is not explicit in the decompilation).

Some numeric constants and animation IDs (0x1B..0x1F, sound ids, created item types/subtypes) are present but their gameplay-facing names/effects are not available here; mapping to exact SFX/animations depends on external data.
Whether any of these scroll handlers also consume the scroll item in unusual ways (beyond Item::destroy at end) across all branches is inferred but could vary by unshown branches.

main_function.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0080
main_function.purpose: When the player reads/uses the 'Scroll of Dispelling Magical Portals', present the scroll text and animations, scan nearby items for sealed/portal doors, clear their sealing quality bit(s) and invoke the door-use routine to open/unseal them; provide visual SFX/animations and player feedback when no targets are affected.
entry_conditions: Player uses or reads the scroll (Scroll::read process is spawned for the item).
Handler runs in the context of the scroll's read/use event while the Scroll::read process is active.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0080
function_map.role: Scroll-of-Dispelling handler (primary entry for dispelling portals).

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func042D
function_map.role: Advance an item's frame incrementally with spawn/suspend delays (visual animation helper).

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func047E
function_map.role: Create/pop a temporary item near a source item, play SFX and animate frames, then destroy temporaries (visual/indicator spawn).

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0520
function_map.role: Handler for 'Detection Scroll of Trapped Chests' — shows text, animates, scans for chests and calls U8P_SCROLL2::func0755 to mark traps.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0755
function_map.role: Create/pop an indicator item at offsets from a chest and animate it; returns success boolean-like result (used by detection flows).

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func07F1
function_map.role: Handler for 'Destruction Scroll of Traps in Chests' — shows text, animates, scans, and calls U8P_SCROLL2::func0A9A to remove/detonate traps.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0A9A
function_map.role: Invoke a method on a target object to perform trap-destruction (spawn target->METHOD::func0676) and return success as process_result() interpreted boolean.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0AF5
function_map.role: Handler for 'Scroll of Healing' — read/stasis/animation and then apply healing via Npc::* calls.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0CC1
function_map.role: Handler for 'Scroll of Restoration' — read/stasis/animation and restoration logic using Npc::getHp/getStr/receiveHitWrapper or setHp.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func0EEE
function_map.role: Handler for 'Scroll of Invisibility' — read/stasis/animation; toggles item status bits to grant temporary invisibility and counts down duration using spawned helpers.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::func11E7
function_map.role: Handler for 'Scroll of Revisibility' — reveal hidden treasure by toggling item status bit (0x10), repopping item, and animating a temporary indicator.

internal_calls.purpose: Animate an item/indicator by advancing frames with timed delays as part of the dispel visual sequence.
Spawn and animate a temporary visual marker/loot near a target when performing the dispel/unseal branch.
When detecting trapped chests, create/pop an indicator near a chest and return success for further handling.
Attempt to perform trap-destruction on a candidate chest by invoking the target's method; use process_result() to detect success.

external_calls.purpose: Close UI windows before showing the scroll text.
Spawn the in-engine scroll text reader and suspend until the player finishes reading.
Freeze/unfreeze the player avatar during the read/animation sequence (stasis on/off).
Spawn short-lived visual/audio helper processes used as timed ticks between animation frame steps.
Request an NPC/object to perform a directional animation/effect used in the scroll effect sequence.
Read and write per-item quality/status words to detect sealed/portal targets and to clear the sealing bit (0x0400) when dispelling.
Invoke the door-use routine on a target door object after clearing sealing bits to effect unsealing/opening (called via spawn/suspend).
Create a temporary item, place it near a source item, animate it by stepping frames, then destroy temporary items.
Guard indicator creation on a quality check and then create/pop/destroy the indicator around a chest target.
Spawn a method on the target object to remove/detonate a trap; the helper returns success based on process_result() after the spawn/suspend.
Compute and apply healing amounts during the Scroll of Healing handler.
Apply restoration effects (either by raising HP to a str-based cap or invoking a receiveHitWrapper with a randomized amount).
Manage invisibility status bits on an item/actor, set timers based on attributes and refresh the item's frame/visual state.
Reveal hidden items by toggling a status bit (0x10), repopping the item at the same coordinates to refresh it, and animating a temporary indicator.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the read/use handlers for a set of in-game magic scrolls. The primary behavior (U8P_SCROLL2::func0080) implements the 'Scroll of Dispelling Magical Portals': it displays the scroll text, freezes the avatar while effects/animations run, scans nearby world objects/items for sealed/portal/door objects (identified by item quality/status bits), attempts to clear a sealing bit on qualifying items and then invokes the door-use routine to open/unseal them, and plays visual/sound effects and short player barks when no applicable targets are found.
